steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s decisive clue is the claim that traffic congestion worsened over time in smaller areas while also being present in large cities.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D states that Miami’s delay was always greater than Boise’s each year, yet the delay rose in both areas from 1990 to 2000, showing that congestion existed in both and increased over time. The other three choices either give a single‑year value, note a low value without a trend, or reverse the size comparison, none of which support the claim of worsening congestion in both large and small areas. That matches choice D — D is correct.
